title: Agentic AI Engineer
notes: Build-altitude track: hands-on implementation of production agentic systems. Owns the fundamentals the higher-level `agentic-systems-architect` track (level 48) links to for depth/architecture framing. Seeded from role research 2026-06-14; job-market evidence and Imagine Learning posting requirements tracked in JOB_REQUIREMENTS.md / .aicg/job-requirements.json. Refine on first nightly research cycle.

modules.title: Agent Fundamentals: The Reason-Act Loop
objectives: Implement the ReAct loop (Thought/Action/Observation) from scratch
Build tool/function calling with Python functions as tools
Handle LLM messages, special tokens, context windows, and token limits
Reason about planning-based vs. reactive agents

modules.title: Agent Frameworks in Practice
objectives: Build the same agent across LangGraph, CrewAI, and AutoGen and compare tradeoffs
Use stateful graph workflows, role-based crews, and conversational patterns
Evaluate and select between competing SDKs (OpenAI Agents SDK, Google ADK, Anthropic Agent SDK, smolagents)
Apply MCP for standardized tool use

modules.title: RAG & Memory Implementation
objectives: Implement retrieval-augmented generation with embeddings and a vector database
Build working, episodic, and long-term memory for agents
Implement just-in-time retrieval and conflict resolution
Build an advanced RAG pipeline (sentence-window / auto-merging retrieval) with evaluation

modules.title: Multi-Agent Systems Implementation
objectives: Implement orchestrator-worker and handoff patterns
Wire inter-agent communication (A2A) and MCP-based tool sharing
Build sub-agent isolation with distilled returns
Implement an evaluator-optimizer self-improvement loop

modules.title: Evaluation & Observability Instrumentation
objectives: Instrument trajectory and tool-call evaluation in code
Wire OpenTelemetry GenAI tracing and a platform (LangSmith/Langfuse/Phoenix)
Implement LLM-as-judge scoring
Build regression eval suites for agents

modules.title: Guardrails & Safety Implementation
objectives: Implement input/output moderation and per-tool-call guardrails (e.g., NeMo Guardrails)
Defend against prompt injection (OWASP LLM01) in code
Enforce least-privilege tool permissions and sandboxing
Add human-approval checkpoints for high-risk actions

modules.title: Productionizing Agents
objectives: Deploy agents behind APIs (FastAPI) with containerization
Implement durable execution and resumption for long-running tasks
Apply prompt caching and model routing for cost/latency
Add human-in-the-loop with state persistence (e.g., LangGraph interrupt)

projects.title: Capstone: Build & Deploy a Production Multi-Agent System
projects.description: Build a deep-research or domain agent (e.g., automated code reviewer, meeting co-pilot) with orchestrator-workers, memory, a tool/function-calling layer, an eval harness, OTel tracing, guardrails, and a deployed API. Taken from build to deployment with a portfolio-grade README.

projects.title: Benchmark Agent (GAIA-style)
projects.description: Build an autonomous multi-tool agent that scores on a public benchmark (GAIA-style: multimodal, tool-use, planning). Includes a reproducible eval run and leaderboard-style reporting.
